focus_items.text: Lila iOS rebuild — working memory home screen is the next milestone.
Anthropic application — cover letter draft circulating with Sarah for feedback.

items.text: Owes line edits on the Anthropic cover letter.

quiet_items.text: The podcast idea from March hasn't moved — still worth keeping warm?

recent_activity.text: Idea: the home screen should be prose Lila wrote about your life, not a database view. Working memory surface.
Sent edits — the second paragraph is the one to rework. Want to talk it through tomorrow?

recent_activity.title: Send Anthropic cover letter + portfolio
recent_activity.note: Got Sarah's edits. Rewriting paragraph 2 today.

recent_activity.text: The Lila branding split — lila.sh and lila.surf — feels right. Two products, one family. Stop relitigating.

recent_activity.title: Schema migration: working_memory table → jsonb columns

recent_activity.text: Jordan asked if I'm in or out on the Mission apartment. Need an answer this week.

recent_activity.title: Reply to Jordan — Mission apartment, in or out
Park lila.surf with the placeholder Cloudflare page

recent_activity.text: Voice rule for the consolidation prompt: sparse is honest. A quiet day produces a quiet screen. Don't pad.
Did you get a chance to look at paragraph 2? Happy to do another pass.

retrieved_memories.text: Matt deeply values being shown receipts when AI makes claims. 'Don't tell me, show me the source record.'
Sarah is Matt's friend and informal editor — sharp on prose, gives line-level feedback.
Matt has been building Lila for ~4 months. The current rebuild is the second swing — first version got too productivity-app.
Captured a podcast idea about persistent operators and AI-native software. Hasn't acted on it.
Jordan is Matt's potential roommate. They've been touring apartments in the Mission together for ~6 weeks.
Anthropic application is one of Matt's two real shots this cycle. Cover letter, not the resume, is the leverage.
Matt locked the lila.sh / lila.surf naming split. Considers it final — does not want to relitigate.
Matt hates SaaS-landing-page voice in product copy. Bans 'leverage', 'optimize', 'actionable'.
